top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The Middle East dominates again: Israel and Iran have just traded fresh blows even as Trump claims he is in the "final throes" of a peace deal, a tanker burns off Oman after a US strike, and a US helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz. A powerful quake in the southern Philippines has killed people with the toll expected to rise. Elsewhere, Xi Jinping wrapped up a rare North Korea visit pledging closer ties, Washington added more Chinese tech and clean-energy firms to a military blacklist, and markets steadied as oil eased on hopes the Gulf crisis is cooling.

stories.headline: Powerful quake hits southern Philippines; death toll expected to rise as aftershocks jolt Mindanao
stories.summary: Rescuers are racing to reach people trapped under collapsed buildings after a strong earthquake struck near General Santos in the southern Philippines. Officials warned the death toll could climb as hundreds of aftershocks continued to shake Mindanao.

stories.headline: Israel-Iran flare-up tests Trump's claim of a Middle East peace deal "in final throes"
stories.summary: A renewed exchange of strikes between Israel and Iran is complicating US-led diplomacy. President Trump said he is in the "final throes" of reaching a Middle East peace deal, while analysts say the flare-up may strengthen Tehran's negotiating hand; Trump also insisted to the BBC that Israeli PM Netanyahu "did not defy him."

stories.headline: All 24 Indian crew rescued from tanker set ablaze off Oman after US strike
stories.summary: India said all 24 sailors aboard a tanker that caught fire off Oman were rescued. The vessel was ablaze following a US strike in the area, near the Strait of Hormuz, where a US military helicopter also crashed; Trump said the pilots were unharmed.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un vow stronger ties as rare North Korea visit wraps up
stories.summary: China's Xi Jinping ended a rare visit to Pyongyang with both leaders pledging deeper cooperation. Xi hailed a "deeper understanding" between the two countries at the close of the summit with North Korea's Kim Jong Un.

stories.headline: US adds Chinese tech, solar and battery firms to military-linked blacklist; Beijing "firmly opposed"
stories.summary: Washington added Chinese companies including BYD, Baidu and Alibaba, along with solar and battery makers, to a list of firms it says aid China's military. Beijing said it was "firmly opposed" to the move.

stories.headline: Asian stocks track Wall Street tech bounce as oil eases on Middle East hopes
stories.summary: Asian equities rose, following a rebound in US tech shares, while oil prices slipped on hopes that tensions in the Gulf may be easing. Markets had been jittery in recent sessions amid tech-sector fears and renewed Middle East attacks.

stories.headline: US judge rules Trump's US$100,000 H-1B visa fee unlawful
stories.summary: A US federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's US$100,000 fee on H-1B skilled-worker visas is unlawful, CNA reported. The decision is a setback for a policy that had alarmed employers and foreign workers.

stories.headline: UK PM Starmer to announce under-16s social media ban within days
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to announce a ban on social media for under-16s within days, The Straits Times reported, in one of the most sweeping moves yet by a major government to restrict youth access to the platforms.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: It's a busy local news day. Singapore Airlines and Southwest will let travellers reach nearly 120 US cities on a single ticket, and Pioneer Generation seniors will get MediSave top-ups of up to S$1,200 in July. Authorities are stepping up the fight against illegal betting ahead of the World Cup — timely, after Mothership reported a man who said he lost S$500,000 on a single basketball game. In buzz, the long-running COCA hotpot at Takashimaya closes on 14 June after 30 years. Expect thundery showers, with highs around 33°C.

stories.headline: SIA-Southwest tie-up connects Singapore to nearly 120 US destinations on one ticket
stories.summary: Singapore Airlines and Southwest Airlines have partnered so passengers can travel to more than 100 US cities on a single ticket, expanding access to nearly 120 American destinations.

stories.headline: Pioneer Generation seniors to get MediSave top-ups of S$300 to S$1,200 in July
stories.summary: Pioneer Generation seniors will receive MediSave top-ups ranging from S$300 to S$1,200 in mid-July, helping with healthcare costs.

stories.headline: Singapore to ramp up enforcement against illegal gambling during World Cup
stories.summary: Police will step up enforcement against illegal and problem gambling during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, targeting unlawful betting operations and warning of the risks of problem gambling.

stories.headline: Singapore to negotiate free trade agreement with 8 East African countries
stories.summary: Singapore will start negotiations on a free trade agreement with a regional bloc of eight East African countries, deepening its trade footprint in Africa.

stories.headline: Residents can post mail from all HDB and condo letterboxes by Sep 30: SingPost
stories.summary: SingPost says residents will be able to post mail from all HDB and condominium letterboxes by 30 September, as it boosts parcel capacity with a new sorting facility. It added there are no plans to remove existing postboxes.

stories.headline: New PMA rules kick in: LTA impounds three devices from errant retailer
stories.summary: With new rules for personal mobility aids (PMAs) now in force, the Land Transport Authority conducted checks and impounded three non-compliant devices from a retailer, signalling tighter enforcement.

stories.headline: Takashimaya hotpot institution COCA to close after 30 years, last day June 14
stories.summary: Long-running hotpot restaurant COCA at Takashimaya will shut after 30 years, with its final day of operations on 14 June, prompting a wave of nostalgia among diners.

stories.headline: Singaporean man says he lost S$500,000 on a single basketball game
stories.summary: Mothership reported on a Singaporean man who said he lost S$500,000 betting on one basketball game, with his girlfriend breaking up with him over his gambling addiction — a cautionary tale circulating as authorities warn about betting ahead of the World Cup. Figures are as recounted by the man to Mothership.

stories.headline: Mercury Festival art and thrift market draws big crowds at Suntec amid virtual-queue debate
stories.summary: The Mercury Festival art and thrift market pulled large crowds at Suntec, sparking online debate over its virtual queue system as shoppers waited for entry.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The Israel-Iran front is the hottest: a tanker burns off Oman after a US strike, a US helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, and fresh Israel-Iran exchanges are unfolding even as Trump touts a near-final peace deal. Israel is also striking Lebanon — hitting Tyre after evacuation warnings — while Lebanon's president pleads for talks over war. On the Russia-Ukraine front there is no battlefield breakthrough; the Kremlin says there are no plans for a Putin-Trump call as Russia holds Baltic naval drills during NATO exercises.

theatres.name: US / Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Active — fresh Israel-Iran exchanges and a US strike off Oman, alongside US-led peace diplomacy

timeline.event: Trump tells the BBC that Israeli PM Netanyahu "did not defy him" amid questions over coordination on the Iran campaign.
A tanker is set ablaze off Oman after a US strike; India says all 24 crew were rescued.
Trump says US helicopter pilots are safe after a crash near the Strait of Hormuz.

Analysts say the latest Israel-Iran flare-up could strengthen Tehran's hand; Trump says he is in the "final throes" of a Middle East peace deal, while reporting notes Israel sought sway over the talks through a brief fight with Iran.

theatres.name: Israel – Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Active — Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon; UN scrutiny of West Bank conduct

timeline.event: Lebanon's president appeals to the Israeli government to pursue talks, not war.
Residents of Lebanon's Tyre flee after an Israeli evacuation warning.
Israel launches deadly strikes on Tyre after the warning, according to The Straits Times.
A UN inquiry finds Israeli forces shield settlers during attacks on Palestinians in the West Bank; Israel rejects such findings.

theatres.name: Russia – Ukraine
theatres.status: Diplomatic stall — no Putin-Trump call planned; Russian naval drills shadow NATO exercises

timeline.event: The Kremlin says there are no plans for a Putin-Trump call and no dates set for US envoys to visit Russia.
Russia holds naval drills in the Baltic Sea during major NATO exercises.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda is the dominant and fast-growing concern: WHO's 8 June report counts 534 confirmed cases and 93 deaths (case-fatality rate ~17%) as of 6 June, a sharp rise partly driven by a testing scale-up. Tensions are spilling over — Kenyan police fired tear gas at a protest against a planned US Ebola quarantine facility. The cruise-ship Andes hantavirus cluster (M/V Hondius) remains small and stable at 13 cases and 3 deaths, with only limited person-to-person spread.

outbreaks.name: Bundibugyo Ebola — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Escalating; spread amplified in health-care settings and unsafe burials
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed cases only, DRC + Uganda combined (DRC 515 cases / 91 deaths; Uganda the balance), as of 6 Jun per WHO DON606 (8 Jun). CFR ~17.4%; figures rose sharply partly due to a testing scale-up and may understate true totals.

outbreaks.name: Andes hantavirus — M/V Hondius cruise cluster
outbreaks.status: Limited person-to-person spread aboard ship; off-ship risk assessed low
outbreaks.scope_note: 11 confirmed + 2 probable cases, 3 deaths (2 confirmed, 1 probable) as of 27 May per WHO DON604 (28 May). Cases identified among passengers/crew from several countries, including Canada, the Netherlands and Spain via follow-up testing.

stories.headline: WHO: Bundibugyo Ebola cases surge to 534 with 93 deaths in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: WHO's latest Disease Outbreak News (8 June) reports 534 confirmed Bundibugyo virus cases and 93 deaths across the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda as of 6 June, a rapid increase since the 29 May update driven in part by expanded testing. At least 17 people have recovered.

stories.headline: Kenyan police fire tear gas at protest against planned US Ebola quarantine facility
stories.summary: Kenyan police used tear gas to disperse protesters opposing a planned US Ebola quarantine facility, as regional anxiety over the Bundibugyo outbreak grows.

stories.headline: WHO: cruise-linked Andes hantavirus cluster holds at 13 cases, 3 deaths
stories.summary: WHO's 28 May update on the hantavirus cluster linked to the M/V Hondius cruise ship reported 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths as of 27 May, with three new confirmed cases since 13 May identified through follow-up testing. Limited person-to-person spread of Andes virus is recognised but no large-scale outbreaks have historically been seen.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Actively exploited flaws lead the cycle. Google shipped an emergency fix for a Chrome V8 zero-day (CVE-2026-11645) under attack, and a critical Check Point VPN bug (CVE-2026-50751, CVSS 9.3) is being exploited as a zero-day and tied to the Qilin ransomware gang — CISA gave US agencies just three days to patch. A LiteLLM flaw (CVE-2026-42271) is being exploited and chains to unauthenticated RCE. Supply-chain attacks continue, with 19 malicious PyPI packages and ongoing worm activity, alongside a string of breaches (French government messaging, SoFi, Oxford University and 20,000 Instagram accounts).

stories.headline: WinRAR flaw exploited by Russia-aligned groups to deploy stealers in Ukraine
stories.summary: Two Russia-aligned campaigns are still exploiting a WinRAR security flaw to target Ukrainian organisations and deploy information stealers, nearly a year after patches were released — underscoring how unpatched archiving tools remain a foothold.

stories.headline: Google patches Chrome V8 zero-day CVE-2026-11645 exploited in the wild
stories.summary: Google released an emergency Chrome update for a V8 JavaScript engine zero-day (CVE-2026-11645) being actively exploited — the fifth Chrome zero-day fixed this year. Users should update immediately.

stories.headline: Researchers build self-replicating AI worm running on local open-weight models
stories.summary: Researchers demonstrated a self-replicating AI worm that operates entirely on local, open-weight models — a proof-of-concept highlighting emerging risks as autonomous AI agents proliferate.

stories.headline: French government messaging service breached in account-hijacking attack
stories.summary: A French government messaging service was breached via an account-hijacking attack, BleepingComputer reported, exposing communications handled through the platform.

stories.headline: Shai-Hulud / Hades supply-chain attack trojanizes 19 science-focused PyPI packages
stories.summary: A new wave of the Shai-Hulud ("Hades") supply-chain attack poisoned 19 science-focused PyPI packages to auto-run a Bun-based credential stealer, continuing a run of open-source registry compromises.

stories.headline: LiteLLM flaw CVE-2026-42271 exploited in the wild, chains to unauthenticated RCE
stories.summary: A command-injection flaw in the popular LiteLLM proxy (CVE-2026-42271) is being exploited in the wild and can be chained to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution. CISA added it to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on 8 June.

stories.headline: NFCShare Android malware spreads via fake banking app updates on GitHub
stories.summary: A new Android malware dubbed NFCShare is spreading through fake banking app updates hosted on GitHub, abusing NFC relay techniques to facilitate fraud, according to BleepingComputer.

stories.headline: SoFi confirms third-party data breach at Hong Kong subsidiary
stories.summary: Financial firm SoFi confirmed a data breach at a Hong Kong subsidiary stemming from a third-party provider, exposing customer information.

stories.headline: One-character Linux kernel flaw enables local root; exploits now public
stories.summary: A Linux kernel bug caused by a single-character error allows local privilege escalation to root, and working exploits are now publicly available, raising the urgency to patch affected systems.

stories.headline: WhatsApp says it disrupted new NSO Group spyware phishing attacks
stories.summary: Meta's WhatsApp said it blocked a new NSO Group spyware phishing campaign and filed a contempt order, the latest round in its long-running legal and security fight with the spyware vendor.

stories.headline: Gogs patches critical zero-day enabling remote code execution
stories.summary: The self-hosted Git service Gogs patched a critical zero-day that allowed remote code execution on affected servers. Administrators are urged to update without delay.

stories.headline: Critical UniFi OS bug lets attackers gain root without authentication
stories.summary: A critical vulnerability in Ubiquiti's UniFi OS allows unauthenticated attackers to gain root access to affected devices, BleepingComputer reported. Patching is strongly advised given the exposure of network gear.

stories.headline: Critical Check Point VPN flaw CVE-2026-50751 exploited as zero-day; CISA orders 3-day patch
stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass flaw in Check Point Security Gateways (CVE-2026-50751, CVSS 9.3) is being exploited as a zero-day in IKEv1 setups to bypass passwords; Check Point linked the attacks to the Qilin ransomware gang. CISA added it to KEV and gave federal agencies three days to patch.
stories.source: BleepingComputer, The Hacker News, CISA KEV

stories.headline: Oxford University discloses data breach after careers platform hack
stories.summary: Oxford University disclosed a data breach following a hack of its careers platform, exposing personal data of affected users.

stories.headline: VerdantBamboo deploys BSD variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux appliances
stories.summary: The threat group VerdantBamboo is deploying a BSD variant of the BRICKSTORM backdoor on Linux appliances, expanding the espionage toolkit's reach onto edge and network devices.

stories.headline: UNC3753 used vishing and physical intrusions in US data-theft extortion campaign
stories.summary: Threat actor UNC3753 combined voice phishing (vishing) with physical intrusions to steal data and extort US organisations, illustrating a blend of social-engineering and in-person tactics.

stories.headline: Over 20,000 Instagram accounts stolen in Meta AI support hack
stories.summary: More than 20,000 Instagram accounts were stolen through a compromise tied to Meta's AI support system, BleepingComputer reported, exposing affected users to account takeover.
